name: San Andres, Providencia and Santa Catalina (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the Caribbean Sea surrounding the archipelago
peace and the islands' historical ties to the Anglo-Caribbean

description: A light blue field with a white saltire from corner to corner.
history: The Archipelago of San Andres, Providencia and Santa Catalina is Colombia's only insular department, lying in the western Caribbean. Its flag, with a white saltire on light blue, evokes both the surrounding sea and the islands' English-speaking Raizal heritage.

features.symbolism: light blue for the Caribbean Sea and white for the saltire emblem of the islands' Anglo-Caribbean heritage
